skill.name: zach-search-term-report-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes Amazon Ads SP, SB, and SD search term reports to identify wasted spend, scale candidates, term categories, listing feedback, and 7, 14, and 30 day trends.
skill.summary: Turns Amazon Ads search term reports into reviewable bid, negative keyword, scale, and listing feedback recommendations.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 448 issues and critical heuristic combinations, but review found no confirmed malicious intent, prompt injection, credential theft, or hidden persistence. The real risks are declared local scripts, optional network fetching of public Amazon listing pages through curl, and broad user-controlled file paths for report inputs and outputs. Publish with a network and local file access warning.

medium_findings.title: Optional Live Amazon Listing Fetch Uses curl
medium_findings.description: The skill can call subprocess.run with curl to fetch public Amazon listing pages. Arguments are passed as a list with no shell, so command injection evidence is not present, but this is real network activity and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess and URL fetch behavior is directly visible in executable code. Risk is moderate because the command is not shell-expanded and targets Amazon listing URLs for declared functionality.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Paths
medium_findings.description: The scripts resolve user-supplied input, output, and listing context paths and can create output directories and files. This supports the advertised workflow but allows broad local file reads and writes if a user supplies sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write paths are explicit in code and are controlled through CLI arguments. This is expected for report processing but remains a meaningful local data handling risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation Backticks Triggered Command Pattern Alerts
low_findings.description: Many static external command findings are markdown code fences, inline field names, or command examples. They document how to run the skill and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown documentation or tables, not executable code. They explain usage and field mappings, so the static Ruby backtick pattern is a false positive here.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto, SAM, and Obfuscation Alerts Are Benign Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak crypto alerts map to ASIN regex patterns, argparse descriptions, Unicode cleanup, and sample HTML text. No hashing, credential extraction, encrypted payload, or Windows SAM access was found in the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain regex literals, Unicode normalization, and product sample HTML. I did not find evidence that these strings perform cryptography, obfuscation, or credential access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Live Amazon Listing Fetch Uses curl
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can call subprocess.run with curl to fetch public Amazon listing pages. Arguments are passed as a list with no shell, so command injection evidence is not present, but this is real network activity and should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess and URL fetch behavior is directly visible in executable code. Risk is moderate because the command is not shell-expanded and targets Amazon listing URLs for declared functionality.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Local File Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts resolve user-supplied input, output, and listing context paths and can create output directories and files. This supports the advertised workflow but allows broad local file reads and writes if a user supplies sensitive paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write paths are explicit in code and are controlled through CLI arguments. This is expected for report processing but remains a meaningful local data handling risk.

content.value_statement: Amazon Ads search term reports can hide wasted spend and missed growth opportunities. This skill converts reports into reviewable actions, trends, and listing feedback.
actual_capabilities: Cleans CSV, XLSX, XLSM, and XLS Amazon Ads search term reports.
Maps common SP, SB, and SD report fields into standard analysis columns.
Builds 7, 14, and 30 day metrics for clicks, spend, orders, CVR, ACOS, and trends.
Classifies terms as brand, competitor, ASIN, category, attribute, scenario, irrelevant, or uncertain.
Generates Markdown reports, CSV details, CSV anomaly lists, and JSON run summaries.
Optionally uses public Amazon listing context or a local listing context file.
limitations: It does not directly change bids, budgets, campaigns, or negative keywords.
Low-volume terms need human review before making advertising changes.
Live listing fetch can fail because of network limits, Amazon blocking, or captcha pages.
Results depend on report field quality and the selected ASIN.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Wasted Spend Review
use_cases.description: Find search terms with high spend, low conversion, or weak listing relevance for manual negative keyword or bid review.

use_cases.title: Find Scale Candidates
use_cases.description: Identify terms with stronger CVR, acceptable ACOS, and improving trends for controlled budget or bid tests.

use_cases.title: Extract Listing Feedback
use_cases.description: Turn customer search language into attribute, scenario, and demand signals for title, bullet, and creative updates.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Report Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Amazon Ads search term report for one ASIN. Show wasted spend, scale candidates, and terms that need manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Offline Listing Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a local listing context file instead of live fetch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this report using my local listing context file. Skip live listing fetch and explain how listing relevance affects each recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Target ACOS Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate terms against a specific profitability target.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this search term report with a 20 percent target ACOS. Separate reduce bid, hold test, and scale up recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Signal Strategy Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for advertising and listing teams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 7, 14, and 30 day search term decision report. Include term categories, trend changes, listing feedback, and review priorities.

output_examples.input: A Sponsored Products search term report for one ASIN with clicks, spend, orders, sales, and search terms.
output_examples.output: A ranked report showing negative keyword candidates, reduce bid candidates, scale candidates, and terms that need observation.

output_examples.input: A report plus a local listing context file containing title, bullet points, and product use cases.
output_examples.output: A listing feedback section with customer language, attribute terms, scenario terms, and relevance notes for manual review.

output_examples.input: A mixed report where several ASINs appear in the same file.
output_examples.output: A blocked or review-needed response asking the user to select one ASIN before generating final recommendations.

best_practices: Analyze one ASIN at a time to avoid mixed baselines.
Use a clear target ACOS when profit rules are already defined.
Review negative keyword and bid recommendations manually before changing campaigns.
anti_patterns: Do not apply recommendations automatically without checking campaign intent.
Do not treat low-click terms as final winners or losers.
Do not rely on live listing fetch when network access is restricted.

faq.question: Does this skill change my Amazon Ads account?
faq.answer: No. It creates analysis files and recommendations only. Users must apply any advertising changes manually.

faq.question: Which report types are supported?
faq.answer: It supports Amazon Ads SP, SB, and SD search term reports when required fields are present.

faq.question: Can it run without internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. Use a local listing context file and skip live listing fetch for deterministic offline analysis.

faq.question: What files does it create?
faq.answer: It can create a Markdown report, detailed CSV, anomaly CSV, and run summary file.

faq.question: How does it classify search terms?
faq.answer: It uses report metrics, ASIN context, brand signals, term patterns, listing relevance, and trend windows.

faq.question: Why is human review still required?
faq.answer: Advertising decisions depend on margins, campaign goals, stock levels, and brand strategy that reports may not contain.
